prose:
**Benzaldehyde Fukui reactivity — computed result**

**Method/provenance.** Property: condensed Fukui indices from Mulliken charges. Level of theory: DFT **B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, chemkit tier `standard`, gas phase. Geometry: user-supplied fixed XYZ at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz` (14 atoms); no geometry optimization was requested in this task. Charge/multiplicity for the neutral reference: **0 / 1**. The N+1 and N−1 states used by the Fukui workflow were returned as anion charge −1 multiplicity 2 and cation charge +1 multiplicity 2. Backend reported by chemkit: `program: dft`; partial charges: **Mulliken (PySCF)**. Solvent: **gas phase**.

**Headline result.** The largest electrophilic Fukui index, f(+), is on **atom 3 (C)**: **0.39938964**. The largest nucleophilic Fukui index, f(−), is on **atom 1 (O)**: **0.23839906**.
